title: The 'I Deserve This' Trap Stealing $4,800/Year
hook: The moment you say 'I deserve this,' your brain has already switched off its spending filter — and that three-word phrase costs the average American $4,800 every single year.
angle: Moral licensing — the psychology behind rewarding yourself after good behavior — is one of the least-talked-about money drains. Doing something virtuous makes your brain feel it's earned permission to splurge, turning every gym visit and diet day into a hidden shopping trigger.
audience: Anyone who's ever justified a purchase as a 'treat yourself' moment after doing something productive or healthy
key_numbers: 73% of Americans regularly justify purchases as self-rewards
$4,800 average annual spend on self-reward purchases
Moral licensing increases splurge likelihood by 40% after virtuous behavior
